raw_title: Research Engineer (Temporary Bonding and Debonding) (APM), IME
title: Research Engineer (Temporary Bonding and Debonding) (APM), IME
raw_description:
As a Research Engineer in the Wafer-to-Wafer (W2W) Bonding Module, you will work on Temporary Bonding and Debonding (TBDB) processes—an essential step in advanced semiconductor packaging for handling ultra-thin wafers. This role focuses on process development, optimization, and stabilization to enable next-generation packaging technologies.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Developing and optimizing temporary bonding and debonding processes for wafer-level packaging.
  • Support integration of process flows, ensuring compatibility across multiple steps in advanced packaging including laser debonding and back grinding.
  • Investigate and resolve process issues and defects such as voids, warpage, and delamination.
  • Plan and execute Design of Experiments (DOE) for process development.
  • Collect, analyze data, and document findings in technical reports.

Job Requirements:

  • Bachelor's degree in Materials Science, Mechanical Engineering, Physics, Chemistry, Electronics, or Electrical Engineering.
  • Experience in TBDB process development or advanced packaging technologies is an advantage.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.
  • Good communication and teamwork skills.
  • Ability to work in a cleanroom environment and handle precision equipment.

title: Electronics Engineer
raw_description:
A Electronics Engineer supports engineering activities such as design, test, verification, modification, fabrication and assembly of prototype electromechanical systems, experimental design circuitry or specialized test equipment.

Responsibilities:

  • Demonstrated ability to independently work from electrical schematics, diagrams, written and verbal descriptions, layouts or defined plans to perform testing, checkout, and trouble-shooting functions.
  • Assemble and test electrical components. This includes mechanical setup of housings, chassis, etc.
  • Work safely with high voltage components in excess of 100V with large energy storage.
  • Demonstrated history of assembling electrical components – PCBA’s, capacitors, FETs, diodes, surface mount, through-hole parts, inductors, etc.
  • Demonstrated soldering skills of small and large electrical components.
  • Operate electrical test equipment to analyze electrical circuits, troubleshoot and measure V, I, impedance, frequency response, etc. Demonstrated history of operation of oscilloscopes, spectrum analyzers, network analyzers, LCR meters in a R&D, non-production-test environment.
  • Knowledge of the function of capacitors, resistors, LRC circuits.
  • Performs operational test and fault isolation on systems and equipment. Assists in determining methods or actions to remedy malfunctions.  Find failed or damaged components within PCBA’s, trace signals to determine electrical circuit issues.
  • Support engineering in writing ECO’s, organizing BOM’s, work orders. Understanding of assembly processes.
  • Occasional documentation of build procedures and tests of electrical components.
  • Document tests, take data, analyze data to determine next actions.
  • Work with electrical component vendors, acquire parts, understanding of PCBA assembly


Experience & Skillsets:

  • Bachelor of Electrical Engineering
  • Has knowledge of basic electrical circuits and common practices in electrical assembly.
  • Must have strong electro-mechanical problem-solving attributes.
  • Strong skills in operation of electrical test equipment – oscilloscopes, network analyzers, spectrum analyzers, volt meters.
  • Manual soldering skills, includes fine pitch components.
  • Good verbal skills to understand instructions, report results

title: Part-Time Fulfilment Officer (B2C Orders)
raw_description:
Job Summary:

We are looking for a meticulous and reliable Part-Time Fulfilment Officer to support order preparation and fulfilment for B2C customer orders. This role is suitable for students who are organised, detail-oriented, and able to follow clear processes in a fast-paced environment.

Key Responsibilities

  • Prepare customer orders accurately based on order lists

  • Verify items, quantities, and labelling before dispatch

  • Organise completed orders neatly for delivery or collection

  • Support basic order fulfilment and operational tasks

  • Assist with simple stock organisation and checking

  • Maintain cleanliness and order within the fulfilment area

  • Follow fulfilment SOPs and safety guidelines

Requirements (Must-Have)

  • Meticulous and detail-oriented (must-have)

  • Responsible, reliable, and punctual

  • Able to follow instructions and processes carefully

  • Comfortable standing for extended periods and doing light lifting

  • No prior experience required (training provided)

Working Hours

  • Part-time, shift-based

  • Weekdays and/or weekends

  • Flexible scheduling to accommodate university timetables


Why Suitable for Students?

  • Flexible shifts (9am to 1pm) or (2pm to 6pm)

  • Hands-on operational experience

  • Clear tasks and structured training provided

  • Short learning curve with transferable workplace skills

raw_description:
We are a centrally located boutique recruitment firm experiencing strong growth. As the company scales, we are looking to bring on a group of Management Associate (Corporate/ Business) who can help support us through the next phase of the growth and development roadmap.


Job Highlights:

  • Start-up environment, welcoming workplace culture
  • Junior/ Entry (No experience required - Training Provided)
  • Career growth and development opportunities
  • Convenient location (walking distance to Tanjong Pagar MRT station)

Job Duties:

  • This role will gain hands-on experience and knowledge of all areas of the business by being assigned to different departments, before being placed in a suitable role permanently.
  • The purpose of the program is to provide the employee with a comprehensive understanding of the business.
  • To participate in business negotiations and building of relationships with core clients and vendors.
  • Assist the directors with the execution on various key business decisions.
  • High-performing individuals can take a fast-track route to a leadership position.
  • There is a possibility to be involved with regional growth or other businesses within the same group.
  • Other ad-hoc duties apply

Description / Requirements:

  • Minimum Degree in Business Administration of Management
  • Good communication and interpersonal skills
  • Excellent time management and organizational skills
  • The potential candidate must be motivated to stay ahead by keeping up with the latest technological advancements and market trends.

Interested candidates please email your resume to hr@recruitpedia.sg / talents@recruitpedia.sg


We regret that only the shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: Talent Acquisition Executive
raw_description:
Fresh Graduates Are Welcome to Apply

Key Responsibilities

Recruitment and Placement

  • Manage the end-to-end recruitment process for frontline positions, including job postings, resume screening, phone interviews, interview coordination, and offer communication.
  • Work closely with business stakeholders to clearly understand job responsibilities and hiring requirements for frontline roles.
  • Develop, maintain, and expand recruitment channels for blue-collar hiring.
  • Effectively utilize both online and offline recruitment platforms, including but not limited to LinkedIn, JobStreet, MyCareersFuture, and on-site job fairs.
  • Establish and manage employee referral programs to encourage internal talent recommendations.
  • Leverage social media platforms (e.g. WeChat, TikTok) to enhance employer branding and promote job opportunities.

Candidate Relationship Management

  • Build strong, professional relationships with candidates and provide a positive, responsive candidate experience.
  • Support offer discussions, coordinate onboarding schedules, and assist with background checks.
  • Track onboarding progress and liaise with hiring managers and administrative teams to ensure smooth employee integration.

Data and Process Optimization

  • Track and analyze recruitment metrics such as interview attendance, offer acceptance rates, and turnover data.
  • Prepare regular recruitment reports and continuously improve recruitment processes to enhance efficiency, quality, and cost control.

Qualifications & Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree or above in Human Resource Management, Psychology, Business Administration, or related disciplines.
  • 1–2 years of relevant recruitment experience preferred; fresh graduates with strong interest in recruitment are welcome to apply.
  • Familiarity with blue-collar recruitment channels and hiring practices in industries such as manufacturing, logistics, or labor-intensive sectors is an advantage.
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage frontline employees from diverse backgrounds.
  • Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and manage multiple priorities effectively.
  • Proactive, responsible, and results-driven mindset.

raw_title: Natural Language Processing Research Fellow (Thai Language Specialist) for AI Singapore (Products)
title: Natural Language Processing Research Fellow (Thai Language Specialist) for AI Singapore (Products)
raw_description:
AI Singapore (AISG) is a national AI programme launched by the National Research Foundation (NRF) to anchor deep national capabilities in Artificial Intelligence (AI).

The programme office is hosted by the Nanyang Technological University and brings together all Singapore-based research institutions and the vibrant ecosystem of AI start-ups and companies developing AI products to perform use-inspired research, grow the knowledge, create the tools, and develop the talent to power Singapore's AI efforts.

We are seeking a highly motivated and talented Research Fellow to join our team and contribute to cutting-edge research in multilingual Large Language Models (LLMs) and Natural Language Processing (NLP), with a specific focus on Southeast Asian languages. This is an exciting opportunity to work in a dynamic and rapidly evolving field, pushing the boundaries of language technology and making a real impact on under-represented languages.

Key Responsibilities:

• Lead research initiatives in pre-training,post-training, alignment, or evaluation

• Define research direction and roadmap within the Hub team

• Design and execute experiments; establish rigorous evaluation frameworks

• Mentor AI Researchers; guide experiment design and research methodology Translate research findings into production-ready improvements for SEA-LION models

• Publish papers, present at conferences, and represent AISG in the research community

• Collaborate with Engineering to ensure research outputs are deployable

• May lead smaller research teams or cross-functional initiatives Accountable for: Research leadership and advancing model capabilities

Job Requirements:

• PhD in Computer Science, Linguistics, Artificial Intelligence, or a related field, with a focus on NLP or machine learning.

• Strong publication track record in top-tier NLP conferences (e.g., ACL, EMNLP, NAACL) and journals (e.g., TACL,JAIR).

• Demonstrated experience in conducting NLP research involving Southeast Asian languages.

• Native fluency in at least one major Southeast Asian language (e.g., Indonesian, Malay, Thai, Vietnamese, Filipino/Tagalog). Fluency in multiple Southeast Asian languages is highly preferred.

• Strong hands-on experience with PyTorch and Hugging Face Transformers library.

• Proven ability to train and fine-tune large language models using GPUs.

• Strong understanding of machine learning and deep learning concepts.

• Ability to work independently and as part of a team.

• Comfortable operating in a rapidly evolving field of research.

• Highly motivated and passionate about NLP and multilingual research.

• Excellent team player with strong interpersonal skills.

• Ability toadapt to changing priorities and deadlines.

• Strong publication record and demonstrated research impact

• Deep expertise in LLM training, alignment, or evaluation

• Ability to balance research exploration with practical delivery

Bonus if you have:

• Knowledge of computational linguistics and phonetics.

• Experience with cloud based computing platforms.

We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: Physiotherapist (Home Based)
raw_description:
Our client is expanding their team. They are looking for a registered Physiotherapist to conduct home based physical therapy sessions for clients.

Job responsibilities

  • Conduct home based physical therapy sessions;
  • Oversee and monitor rehabilitative exercise programmes for individual clients;
  • Develop and implement appropriate programs to improve the overall wellbeing of the client;
  • To be able to support neuro related cases and geriatric type clients;
  • Provide advice and protocols in conducting rehabilitative exercise programmes;
  • Provide clinical advice on handling of clients with chronic illnesses;
  • Work with Exercise Specialist to execute programmes;
  • Maintain database/records of all clients to provide evidence-based exercise programme with accurate and appropriate documentation;
  • Ensure proper case notes for all clients;
  • Collate data from outcome measures and provide review and analysis of data;
  • Any other duty as assigned by Director of the team or Lead Physiotherapist.

Work schedule: Tuesday to Saturday
Travel expense is covered.

Requirements

  • Obtained Full or Restricted Registration and a valid practising License with the Singapore Allied Health Professional Council (AHPC);
  • Degree or Diploma from a recognised institution;
  • Preferably 2 or 3 years clinical experience;
  • Be a team player with excellent communication skills;
  • Able to work independently;
  • Willingness to learn;
  • Experience in geriatric and/or neuro cases.

For clarifications, please contact Karyn Choo (EA Personnel Reg: R24120779) karyn@jobsjolt.com
Employment Agency: Jobs Jolt Private Limited

EA Licence No.: 24C2131

title: Clinical Associate
raw_description:
The National University Health System (NUHS) is an integrated Academic Health System and Regional Health System in Singapore that delivers value-driven, innovative and sustainable healthcare. Institutions in the NUHS group include three hospitals – National University Hospital, Ng Teng Fong General Hospital and Jurong Community Hospital; three National Specialty Centres – National University Cancer Institute, Singapore, National University Heart Centre, Singapore and National University Centre for Oral Health, Singapore; a polyclinic group – the National University Polyclinics; one medical centre – Jurong Medical Centre; and three academic health sciences institutions – National University of Singapore (NUS) Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine (including the Alice Lee Centre for Nursing Studies), NUS Faculty of Dentistry and NUS Saw Swee Hock School of Public Health.


The Departments are seeking candidates who are highly motivated and willing to join us for a challenging and fulfilling appointment as Clinical Associate on a contractual basis.


As a clinical associate, you will be responsible for the total management of patients in the ward and administrative duties required of the department to provide direct patient care. In addition, you will be responsible for assisting the Registrar and Senior Staff in the running of the Specialist Outpatient Clinics.

  • Be in active clinical practice
  • Possess a basic medical degree registrable with the Singapore Medical Council
  • Possess a 3 year of post housemanship clinical experience
  • Must be registered with the Singapore Medical Council
  • Able to work independently with demonstrated competence in managing patients in the department
  • Confident, takes initiatives and a good team player

title: Product Development Engineer
raw_description:
THE ROLE: 

As a Product Development engineer, you will collaborate with various Product Development Groups to bring-up and ramp new products which include Plan, debug and handling manufacturing and characterization test programs for next-generation AMD microprocessors. 

THE PERSON: 

A successful candidate is one who can perform in a role that requires accountability for accuracy, reliability and completeness of assignment results critical to the project’s success.  

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: 

  • Validate new product features, test conditions, test methodologies, and test contents during first silicon bring-up 
  • Post Silicon Bring up of Test patterns leading to optimization for mass production enablement 
  • Analysis of part failures leading to test improvement 
  • Test pattern & test flow development, debug, test and characterization 

PREFERRED EXPERIENCE: 

  • Knowledge of scripting languages such as Perl, Ruby, shell script 
  • Good understanding of Linux, Unix or Windows OS 
  • ATE (Automatic Test Equipment) test experience 

ACADEMIC CREDENTIALS: 

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s in electrical engineering, computer engineering, or comparable disciplines

Overview

We are seeking a detail-oriented and motivated Accounts Assistant to support our finance operations. The ideal candidate will assist with daily accounting tasks, maintain accurate financial records, and ensure timely processing of transactions.

Key Responsibilities

  • Assist with data entry of daily financial transactions into the accounting system

  • Prepare and process invoices, receipts, and payments

  • Ability to work well independently and as part of a team

Additional Skills (Optional)

  • Experience with GST submissions or payroll processes

  • Familiarity with Singapore accounting standards (if applicable)

  • Good communication and interpersonal skills

title: Junior Finance & Trade Admin Executive
raw_description:
Role Overview

We are a Singapore-based commodities trading group seeking a Junior Finance & Trade Admin Executive to support our finance and trading operations.

This role focuses on trade finance documentation, banking administration, and finance support, with secondary responsibility for general office administration and coordination. It is a hands-on support role suited for a detail-oriented individual who is comfortable working with documentation, banks, and internal stakeholders in a fast-paced trading environment.

Key Responsibilities

Trade Finance & Trade Administration (Primary)

  • Prepare, compile, and submit KYC / CDD documentation to banks and trading counterparties

  • Handle trade documentation including Bills of Lading (BL), invoices, packing lists, certificates, and other shipping documents

  • Coordinate with banks on:

    • Trade finance documentation (e.g. LC, trust receipts, scan BL submissions)

    • Facility utilisation requests and document follow-ups

  • Maintain well-organised and audit-ready trade documentation records

  • Track trade documentation from pre-shipment to post-shipment stages

Finance Support

  • Assist with basic bookkeeping support, reconciliations, and invoice processing

  • Support payment and collection tracking

  • Maintain simple cashflow and trade tracking spreadsheets

  • Provide administrative support to the finance team as required

Office Administration (Secondary)

  • Coordinate office service providers (cleaning, pantry, stationery, IT vendors)

  • Manage office supplies and general filing

  • Support basic HR administration such as leave tracking and onboarding documentation

  • Ensure smooth day-to-day office operations

Requirements

  • Diploma or Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business, Logistics, or a related field

  • 1–3 years of relevant working experience, preferably in:

    • Commodity trading or trading support roles

    • Trade finance or banking operations

    • Shipping, logistics, or documentation-focused roles

  • Familiarity with trade documentation and banking processes is an advantage

  • Proficient in Microsoft Excel and comfortable working with documentation

  • Strong attention to detail and good organisational skills

  • Able to work independently and manage multiple tasks

  • Comfortable in a support role with a mix of finance and administrative responsibilities

Salary & Benefits

  • Monthly Salary Range: SGD 3,200 – 4,000

  • Salary commensurate with experience and skills

  • Stable role with exposure to commodities trading and trade finance operations

Reporting Line

  • Reports directly to the Head of Finance

title: Legal Associate
raw_description:
About the Role

We are expanding our Singapore legal team and are seeking a Legal Associate to support a group of Singapore-qualified lawyers advising on sophisticated and cross-border financial services and FinTech matters. This role offers hands-on exposure to FinTech advisory, licensing, and regulatory work, including matters relating to derivatives broking, payments and financial markets activities. The Singapore team is part of a broader regional and global group of legal and compliance professionals.

Key Responsibilities

  • Support Legal Counsels on a wide range of FinTech, payments and financial services legal matters.
  • Assist with licensing, regulatory advisory work, and contractual matters across multiple jurisdictions.
  • Work on legal and regulatory issues relating to derivatives broking, payments and financial markets.
  • Draft, review, and analyse legal documentation, policies, and regulatory submissions.
  • Conduct legal research and provide practical, solution-oriented support.
  • Work collaboratively with legal, compliance, and business teams as part of a wider function.

What We're Looking For

  • Bachelor's degree in Law from Singapore or other jurisdictions; bar qualification is a bonus.
  • Early-career lawyers or paralegals with approximately 2-3 years’ experience in financial services, capital markets, or regulatory practices; fresh graduates will also be considered.
  • Positive attitude, strong work ethic, and willingness to play a supportive role within a wider team.
  • Strong interest in FinTech, financial regulation, payments and derivatives.
  • Good legal research, drafting, and analytical skills.
  • Reliable, detail-oriented, and comfortable in a fast-paced environment.
  • Prior experience in a law firm or in-house financial services team is an advantage.

What We Offer

  • Join a high-calibre senior Singapore legal team within a wider regional and global legal & compliance group.
  • Exposure to complex, cross-border FinTech, payments and financial markets work.
  • Strong learning opportunities and career development.
  • Collaborative, professional team culture that values attitude and contribution.

title: CRM Support Specialist
raw_description:
Company Overview

ESET® provides cutting-edge digital security to prevent attacks before they happen. By combining the power of AI and human expertise, ESET stays ahead of known and emerging cyberthreats — securing businesses, critical infrastructure, and individuals. Whether it’s endpoint, cloud or mobile protection, our AI-native, cloud-first solutions and services remain highly effective and easy to use. Driven by science, ESET technology includes robust detection and response, ultra-secure encryption, and multifactor authentication. With 24/7 real-time defense and strong local support, we keep users safe and businesses running without interruption. An ever-evolving digital landscape demands a progressive, evidence-based approach to security. ESET is committed to world-class scientific research and powerful threat intelligence, backed by R&D centers and a strong global partner network.

ESET's global headquarters are in Bratislava, Slovakia, supporting its worldwide operations. The Asia-Pacific (APAC) region is managed from ESET’s regional headquarters in Singapore, with local offices in Tokyo, Sydney, and the SAARC region and is represented by an extensive global partner network.

Get to know us better – ESET.com, YouTube, Twitter, LinkedIn, Great Place To Work

General Position Overview

In APAC, our fast-growing, multicultural team spans Singapore, India, and Australia—working together across time zones to support customers and colleagues with resilience, integrity, and innovation.
We are now looking for a Systems Support Specialist to join our APAC team. This role is perfect for someone who enjoys solving problems, supporting internal users, and working with business systems (including CRM/Salesforce).

Job Description
Daily Support & User Engagement

  • Provide first-line and second-line support for business systems through tickets, walk-ups, and virtual channels
  • Communicate with end-users to clarify incidents and requests, ensuring timely and accurate resolution
  • Investigate, troubleshoot, and resolve system incidents and user queries

CRM & Systems Operations

  • Support CRM administration (Salesforce experience is a plus)
  • Assist in reviewing, testing, and validating new or modified business systems
  • Develop test data, evaluate results, and provide structured feedback

Documentation & Process Improvement

  • Create, maintain, and distribute user manuals, guides, and support documentation
  • Maintain and enhance system support processes and knowledge bases
  • Monitor and report on incident trends to help improve service quality

Requirements

  • Bachelor's degree in Information Systems or a related field
  • 2 years of experience in business systems support or application support
  • Experience with Salesforce or CEM platform would be advantageous
  • Fluent in business English
  • Strong time management and ability to prioritize in a multi-country environment
  • Comfortable working in cross-functional, multicultural teams
  • Reliable, detail-oriented, proactive, and willing to learn

We are seeking a QA Tester who will be will be responsible for conducting tests before product launches to ensure software runs smoothly and meets client needs. The QA engineer responsibilities include designing and implementing tests, debugging and defining corrective actions. You will also review system requirements and track quality assurance metrics (e.g. defect densities and open defect counts.

  • Maintain automated scripts utilizing the existing framework, adhering to coding standards
  • Participate in troubleshooting and drive root cause analysis and resolution
  • Analyze and identify defects from regression test report
  • Record, document and track bugs that detected by automation script
  • Perform other duties as assigned

Requirements

  • Diploma/Degree in Computer Science, Engineering or a related subject
  • Strong knowledge of software QA methodologies, tools and processes

title: Administrative Assistants
raw_description:
Required Qualifications

  • Passionate about the cause of education
  • Dedicated and patient
  • Team spirit
  • Bachelor's Degree in the related subject

Job Timings (Non-negotiable)

  • 44 Hours Per Week
  • Monday - Thursday: 11:30 am - 07:30 pm
  • Saturday and Sunday: 09:30 am - 06:30 pm
  • Lunch Break: 01:30 pm - 02:30 pm

Overtime

Employees who work more than the stipulated working hours on any day will be paid for any additional hour worked at 1.5 times their hourly basic rate of pay.

Public Holidays

  • The Centre will remain closed on all public holidays EXCEPT Labour Day and Vesak Day. The board examinations are held in May/June and we are duty bound to our students to remain open on these two public holidays to assist them in their final examination preparation.
  • Employees who work on Public Holidays (9:30AM - 6:30PM) will receive twice their basic rate of pay for work on that day.

Leave Policy

  • Annual leave : 18 days
  • Birthday Leave: 1 day
  • Family Care Leave : 2 days (the above unused leave will be encashed at the end of the year)
  • Medical leave: 14 days
  • Hospitalisation Leave : 60 days (includes 14 days of medical leave) Unused leaves will lapsed at the end of the year
  • Maternity leave :16 weeks (For Singapore Citizens) 8 weeks (For others)
  • Paternity leave :2 weeks (If employee has worked for 3 continuous months and child is a Singapore Citizen) For Others, as per mutual agreement between employee and employer.
  • Childcare Leave :6 days (if child is less than 7 years, and a Singapore Citizen) 2 days (if child is less than 7 years, for others, as per Employment Act)
  • Compassionate Leave: 3 days (can be availed only once in a year)

Insurance

  • General Physician (GP) : No Co-Pay (Panel Clinic)
  • Specialist (SPL) : $2000 per calendar year (No Panel)
  • Hospitalisation (Hosp) : $60000 Annual (Govt Restr, 4 bedded Ward)
  • Dental Plan (DP) : $1000 per calendar year, 10% Co-Pay (No Panel)

Resignation Procedure

  • Employees are free to resign with just a 1-day notice period. We understand that new opportunities can arise, and we don’t want a lengthy notice period to stand in your way. Our priority is to support your success, whether that’s with us or in your next role.

Walk-in interview

Monday Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Saturday and Sunday at 2:30PM at MathVision Campus, Tekka Place, Annex Block, #01-51 to #01-54 and #01-55 to #01-59, 2 Serangoon Road, Singapore.

title: Admin Associate / Executive (Social Service)
raw_description:
Monday to Friday

8:00 AM – 5:30 PM

Location: Toa Payoh/ Elizabeth Drive

Job Responsibilities

  • Gather social and financial background information of residents and/or their families prior to the application of financial assistance schemes.
  • Update and maintain accurate records in the referral and enquiries database and information system.
  • Ensure timely submission of financial assistance applications such as Medifund, MFEC (Medical Fee Exemption Card), IDAPE (Interim Disability Assistance Programme for the Elderly), PGDAS (Pioneer Generation Disability Assistance Scheme), and others.
  • Act as the main point of contact and liaison between residents, families, and healthcare institutions to coordinate referrals and process applications for potential residents.
  • Ensure proper documentation of case files, referrals, and other relevant documents for effective case management and audit purposes.
  • Perform data entry and regularly update information to maintain accuracy of databases.
  • Handle administrative tasks including enquiries, referrals, and admissions.
  • Facilitate residents’ transfers to other facilities or healthcare institutions, as well as coordinate discharge processes.
  • Undertake any other duties or projects as assigned by the supervisor.
  • May required to be stationed in the office to handle incoming phone calls, walk-in enquiries, and other front-desk administrative duties as and when required

Requirements

  • Minimum 1 year of relevant working experience in social service, community service, healthcare, or related sectors.
  • Prior exposure to financial assistance schemes (e.g. Medifund, MFEC, IDAPE, PGDAS) will be an advantage
  • Diploma or Degree in Social Work, Social Sciences, Community Services, Healthcare Administration, or related disciplines preferred.
  • Comfortable liaising with residents, families, healthcare institutions, and external agencies.
  • Strong administrative and documentation skills, with attention to detail for case files and audit purposes.

Candidates pursuing a part-time diploma or degree in a relevant field (e.g. Social Work or Psychology) are welcome to apply


Interested candidates please WhatsApp me at 8779 6100 or send in your resume to willie.goh@swiftsearchglobal.com

We regret to inform you that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

Willie Goh | EA Reg No: R2197853

Swift Search Global Pte Ltd | EA No: 18C9495

title: Design Engineer (ASIC)
raw_description:
Responsibilities:

  • Assist in designing micro-architecture for ISP and CV algorithms.
  • Perform RTL design using Verilog/System Verilog and HLS tools (Catapult).
  • Support the analysis of metrics related to power, performance, and area.
  • Collaborate with architects and DV Engineers to ensure efficient verification signoff.
  • Help with FPGA tasks, including emulation, validation, and debugging.


Qualification/ Requirements:

  • Education: Minimum Master’s degree and above in Electrical/Electronics/Computer Engineering.
  • Experience: Entry-level position; relevant coursework or project experience in ASIC design or image signal processing is a plus.

Technical Skills:

o Experience/knowledge in Verilog/SystemVerilog and C/C++.

o Familiarity with ASIC design processes and frontend design.

o Knowledge of image/vision/video data processing or algorithm acceleration is beneficial.

o Familiarity with scripting languages (Python, Perl, Tcl) is a plus.


Soft Skills: Strong teamwork and communication skills.

Additional Notes: Strong analytical skills and a willingness to learn and adapt in a dynamic environment.

raw_title: Research Associate (Audio LLM) for AI Singapore (Products)
title: Research Associate (Audio LLM) for AI Singapore (Products)
raw_description:
AI Singapore (AISG) is a national AI programme launched by the National Research Foundation (NRF) to anchor deep national capabilities in Artificial Intelligence (AI).

The programme office is hosted by the Nanyang Technological University and brings together all Singapore-based research institutions and the vibrant ecosystem of Institutes of Higher Learning (IHLs) in developing AI products to perform use-inspired research, grow the knowledge, create the tools, and develop the talent to power Singapore's AI efforts.

We are looking for a Research Associate for the Audio LLM development efforts of the SEA-LION project under AI Singapore. The successful candidate will help develop and train foundation/large audio language models, build datasets/pipelines for data development, evaluation benchmarks and developer enablement tools for the SEA-LION audio initiatives.

Key Responsibilities:

• Contribute to algorithm design, training and serving SEA-LION team’s LLM audio products.

• Participate in ongoing integration and optimization with entities both in AI Singapore and external collaborators to develop AI Singapore’s LLM solutions.

• Conduct literature review, identify relevant state-of-the-art techniques for multimodal LLMs (audio and text).

• Develop data processing pipeline for multilingual audio datasets.

• Keep abreast of latest developments in NLP, AI and LLMs.

Job Requirements:

• Masters or PhD in computer science, machine learning, AI, and other equivalent relevant fields.

• Familiar with PyTorch, Large Language Model’s Training and Data pipelines.

• Data driven, creative, and resourceful.

• Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.

• Able to proactively identify opportunities, take initiative and multi-task in a fast paced environment.

• Background in IT disciplines, working with a wide variety of tools and solutions.

Up-to-date with AI research areas and current trends, especially their application to solving problems.

• Bonus if you have:

• Contribution to past open-source projects.

• Experience as a computer engineer working within a small/medium organization.

• Additional interest in Natural Language Processing (NLP).

We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

raw_title: Senior / Engineer, Data Center Facilities Management (1-Net)
title: Senior / Engineer, Data Center Facilities Management (1-Net)
raw_description:
COMPANY DESCRIPTION

1-Net manages carrier-neutral Internet Data Centers, along with providing a comprehensive range of integrated services including network connectivity, managed services, work area recovery suite, cloud and media delivery services. 1-Net's Data Center Corridor initiative currently interconnects key data centers in Singapore to facilities quick deployment and providing a diverse connectivity options for enterprises and service providers.

1-Net is fast transforming itself into Asia's Data Center of Choice. As one of the pioneers in broadband development in Singapore, 1-Net currently manages telco-class carrier-neutral Internet Data Centers, along with providing domestic and international connectivity, managed services and media delivery services. 1-Net offers superior stability, secure delivery and service experience through its services. It's uniquely positioned in Southeast Asia, with a comprehensive range of integrated services and a media-centric approach to bring cutting edge solutions to its customers.

We are committed to creating an inclusive and diverse workplace where talent thrives. Our hiring decisions are made based on merit and fit-to-role. If you have a disability or special need which requires accommodation to participate in the recruitment process, please inform us when you submit your online application. We will be happy to support as necessary.

Thank you for your interest and application to this role. Please note that only short-listed candidates will be contacted.

RESPONSIBILITIES

Facilities Operation and Maintenance:

  • Conduct the required daily maintenance and operations of the data center facilities to ensure serviceability of all active and redundancy equipment. This includes performing routine facilities activities such as critical system checks and functional testing
  • Respond to Mechanical and Electrical alarms/faults, identify trends, and implement corrective actions as needed
  • Respond and investigate Building Management System (BMS)/ Environmental Monitoring System (EMS) alerts, providing feedback on outstanding issues or events, responding to emergency situations, and being on standby duty as required
  • Support Data Center projects and any additional and alteration work such as site construction activities and installations, coordination with external contractors and testing in adherence to operational standards within the given timeline
  • Other duties as assigned that are consistent with the responsibilities and objectives of the role

Technical and Customer Support:

  • Ensure contracted service level agreements and key performance indicators are met or exceeded
  • Attend to customer requests and deliveries such as installations, coordination with the design & construction, operations requirements to coordinate customer move-in and turn-on, and others

Events and Accreditation Support:

  • Involve in the scheduling, coordination and completion of all significant planned and emergency maintenance events for the facility and ensure these activities are executed in a controlled and proven method
  • Undertake assigned tasks to support the various accreditation, certification and compliance initiatives such as BCA-IDA Green Mark DC/QMS/ISMS/BCMS/PCIDSS/OSPAR, etc as may be required
  • Comply to all standard operating procedures (SOP), method of procedures (MOP), emergency operating procedures (EOP) and emergency response procedures (ERP) established for the critical environments
QUALIFICATIONS
  • We welcome the following applicants:
    • Degree graduates in Electrical or Mechanical engineering or equivalent qualifications
    • Candidates without the required academic credentials but with at least 2 years of relevant working experience in Data Center facilities management and operations or facility management experiences (M&E) in building, construction, semiconductors or equivalent
    • Candidates with more years of relevant working experience in Data Center facilities management and operations may be considered for the Senior Engineer role
  • Good technical and functional skills and knowledge of facilities management and operations
  • Able to commit to 24x7 rotating shift duty
  • Strong communication skills
  • Working location: 1-Net (East) at Chai Chee / 1-Net (North) at Woodlands
Please note that your application will be sent to and reviewed by the direct employer - 1-Net Singapore Pte Ltd

title: Desk Quant Analyst
raw_description:
Squarepoint is a global investment management firm that utilizes a diversified portfolio of systematic and quantitative strategies across financial markets that seeks to achieve high quality, uncorrelated returns for our clients. We have deep expertise in trading, technology and operations and attribute our success to rigorous scientific research. As a technology and data-driven firm, we design and build our own cutting-edge systems, from high performance trading platforms to large scale data analysis and compute farms. With offices around the globe, we emphasize true, global collaboration by aligning our investment, technology and operations teams functionally around the world.


Overview of Desk Quant Analyst Position:

  • Maintain and upgrade the codebase and configuration of strategies within the firm’s automated trading framework.
  • Manage large datasets used in both research and production environments.
  • Perform live as well as historical monitoring of trading activities. For instance, do some post-trade analysis or production reconciliation.
  • Work in close collaboration with quant researchers and traders to understand the constantly evolving needs of the trading desks.

Typical Day of Desk Quant Analyst:

  • Before market open, check that all required data and related processes are ready for the trading day.
  • During market hours, sporadically monitor behavior and performance of strategies.
  • Work on various longer-term projects required for expanding our systematic trading activities and ensuring their robustness.

Skill Set Required For Position:

  • Degree in Engineering, Computer Science or related technical subject.
  • Programming proficiency with at least one major programming or scripting language (e.g. C++, Java, Python).
  • Great attention to details.
  • Strong communication skills and ability to work well with colleagues across multiple regions.
  • Ability to work well under pressure.

Additional Desirable Qualifications:

  • Knowledge of Q/KDB+.
  • Knowledge of Linux and Bash.
  • Advanced quantitative skills.

title: Junior Business Development Executive (Fresh Graduate)
raw_description:
Job description

Salary: 50,000.00 (SGD) - 70,000.00 (SGD) per year


Job description:


International School Group – Singapore HQ Focus: Japan, Thailand & Southeast Asia (SEA) Expansion


Position Overview

We are looking for a motivated fresh graduate to join our Singapore-based international school group as a Junior Business Development Manager. This entry-level role is designed for high-potential talent eager to launch a career in global education expansion, with a focus on Japan, Thailand, and Southeast Asia (SEA).You will support senior leaders in researching new school opportunities, building partnerships, and laying the groundwork for campus launches in high-growth markets. This is a structured 18–24 month accelerated development program with mentorship, regional travel, and clear progression to full Business Development Manager within 2 years.


Location: Singapore Start Date: Q1 2026 Salary: SGD 50,000 – 70,000 per annum + performance bonus (up to 2 months)

Travel: 20–30% (Japan, Thailand, Vietnam, Indonesia, Malaysia)


Key ResponsibilitiesMarket Intelligence & Research

Conduct desktop and field research on education regulations, demographics, and competitor schools in Japan (Tokyo, Osaka), Thailand (Bangkok, Chiang Mai), and SEA cities.


Build and maintain a live database of potential partners: local schools, real estate developers, government bodies, and investor groups.


Partnership Support

Assist in outreach to local education ministries, MOUs, and joint-venture discussions.
Prepare pitch decks, feasibility summaries, and data rooms for senior review.


Project Coordination

Support end-to-end project timelines: from site visits and due diligence to pre-launch marketing plans.
Coordinate with curriculum, admissions, and operations teams in Singapore.


Networking & Representation

Attend education fairs, agent briefings, and regional conferences (e.g., EARCOS, ISAT, BETT Asia) as a junior delegate.
Represent the brand in agent training sessions and parent information events.


Analytics & Reporting

Track KPIs: lead generation, conversion rates, market entry timelines.
Prepare monthly dashboards and board updates using Google Sheets, Tableau, or CRM tools.


Language & Cultural Bridge

Leverage Japanese, Thai, or SEA language skills (if any) to support translation and relationship building.
Participate in cultural immersion trips (e.g., 1-week homestay in rural Thailand or school shadowing in Japan).


Who We’re Looking For (Fresh Graduates)Education

Bachelor’s degree (any discipline) from a recognized university
Preferred majors: Business, International Relations, Education, Marketing, Japanese Studies, Southeast Asian Studies
Final-year GPA: 3.4/4.0 or Second Upper Honours (or equivalent) Experience
No prior full-time work required
Valued (not required):
Internship in education, real estate, consulting, or startup environments
Student leadership (e.g., club president, event organizer)
Study abroad or exchange in Japan, Thailand, or SEA


Skills & Traits

Research & Analytical: Comfortable with Excel, Google Workspace, and basic data visualization
Communication: Clear writer and confident presenter (English fluency required)
Languages (bonus, not mandatory):
Japanese (JLPT N3 or higher)
Thai, Bahasa Indonesia/Malaysia, or Vietnamese
Adaptability: Thrives in ambiguity; eager to learn fast in a dynamic environment
Cultural Curiosity: Genuine interest in Asian education systems and family decision-making


What We Offer

Fast-Track Career: Clear path to mid-level role in 2 years
Regional Exposure: Work and travel across Japan, Thailand, Vietnam, Malaysia
Wellness & Growth:
Annual learning budget (SGD 1,500)
16 days annual leave + 1 study leave day per quarter


How to Apply

Email: careers@kbh.edu.sg

We are an equal opportunity employer. Singapore citizens, PRs, and international graduates with employment pass eligibility are welcome


Job Type: Full-time

Benefits:Health insurance

title: FINANCIAL CONSULTANT
raw_description:
Join one of Singapore’s most dynamic investment firms as it expands its exclusive private client division, in partnership with global investment leaders BlackRock, Fidelity, and Schroders. Backed by a strong private equity heritage, the firm delivers institutional-grade strategies to high-net-worth and ultra-high-net-worth clients seeking access to opportunities typically reserved for large institutions. This position offers a rare platform to craft sophisticated investment solutions, foster enduring client relationships, and contribute to a fast-growing firm redefining wealth management in Asia.

Preferred

  • Recent graduates, Financial Advisors, Relationship Managers, Private Bankers welcomed.
  • A background in investment analysis, portfolio management, or a related field in a private bank, asset management firm, or multi-family office.
  • Demonstrable experience in constructing and managing multi-asset class portfolios, with exposure to equities, fixed income, and alternative investments.
  • Practical experience in interpreting and applying institutional-grade investment research from leading global asset managers to inform strategy.
  • Experience within private banking or wealth management, with a verifiable track record of managing and growing a portfolio of HNW/UHNW relationships. The role is further supported by a consistent stream of qualified leads from the firm's established corporate partnerships.
  • A background that demonstrates exceptional interpersonal skills and the professional gravitas required to build rapport with entrepreneurs, C-suite executives, and multi-generational families.
  • A proven ability to deliver a superior service experience, with outstanding communication skills and a meticulous attention to client service detail.

Required

  • Will need to obtain or posses minimum 4 MAS papers
  • Singaporean Permanent Resident or Citizen only
